question_results.question: How much of the hydrogen in molecule TR206 is accounted for? Please provide your answer as a percentage with four decimal places.
question_results.gold_sql: SELECT ROUND(CAST(COUNT(CASE WHEN T.element = 'h' THEN T.atom_id ELSE NULL END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T.atom_id),4) FROM atom AS T WHERE T.molecule_id = 'TR206'
question_results.predicted_sql:
SELECT printf('%.4f', CASE WHEN COUNT(a.atom_id)=0 THEN 0.0000 ELSE (SUM(CASE WHEN a.element = 'h' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0) / COUNT(a.atom_id) END) AS percent
FROM atom AS a
WHERE a.molecule_id = 'TR206'
question_results.normalized_sql:
SELECT
  PRINTF(
    '%.4f',
    CASE
      WHEN COUNT(a.atom_id) = 0
      THEN 0.0000
      ELSE (
        SUM(CASE WHEN a.element = 'h' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0
      ) / COUNT(a.atom_id)
    END
  ) AS percent
FROM atom AS a
WHERE
  a.molecule_id = 'TR206'

question_results.question: Of the schools that offers a magnet program serving a grade span of Kindergarten to 8th grade, how many offers Multiple Provision Types? question_results.gold_sql: SELECT T2.City, COUNT(T2.CDSCode) FROM frpm AS T1 INNER JOIN schools AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.CDSCode WHERE T2.Magnet = 1 AND T2.GSoffered = 'K-8' AND T1.`NSLP Provision Status` = 'Multiple Provision Types' GROUP BY T2.City

  (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT s2.CDSCode)
   FROM schools AS s2
   JOIN frpm AS f2 ON f2.CDSCode = s2.CDSCode
   WHERE s2.Magnet = 1
     AND (s2.GSserved = 'K-8' OR s2.GSoffered = 'K-8')
     AND f2."NSLP Provision Status" = 'Multiple Provision Types'
  ) AS magnet_multiple_provision_count,
  (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT s3.City)
   FROM schools AS s3
   WHERE s3.City IS NOT NULL
     AND (s3.GSserved = 'K-8' OR s3.GSoffered = 'K-8')
  ) AS total_k8_cities,
  (SELECT group_concat(city_count, '; ')
   FROM (
     SELECT s4.City || ':' || COUNT(s4.CDSCode) AS city_count
     FROM schools AS s4
     WHERE s4.City IS NOT NULL
       AND (s4.GSserved = 'K-8' OR s4.GSoffered = 'K-8')
     GROUP BY s4.City
     ORDER BY COUNT(s4.CDSCode) DESC
   )
  ) AS k8_city_counts
question_results.normalized_sql:
SELECT
  (
    SELECT
      COUNT(DISTINCT s2.CDSCode)
    FROM schools AS s2
    JOIN frpm AS f2
      ON f2.CDSCode = s2.CDSCode
    WHERE
      s2.Magnet = 1
      AND (
        s2.GSserved = 'K-8' OR s2.GSoffered = 'K-8'
      )
      AND f2."NSLP Provision Status" = 'Multiple Provision Types'
  ) AS magnet_multiple_provision_count,
  (
    SELECT
      COUNT(DISTINCT s3.City)
    FROM schools AS s3
    WHERE
      NOT s3.City IS NULL AND (
        s3.GSserved = 'K-8' OR s3.GSoffered = 'K-8'
      )
  ) AS total_k8_cities,
  (
    SELECT
      GROUP_CONCAT(city_count, '; ')
    FROM (
      SELECT
        s4.City || ':' || COUNT(s4.CDSCode) AS city_count
      FROM schools AS s4
      WHERE
        NOT s4.City IS NULL AND (
          s4.GSserved = 'K-8' OR s4.GSoffered = 'K-8'
        )
      GROUP BY
        s4.City
      ORDER BY
        COUNT(s4.CDSCode) DESC
    )
  ) AS k8_city_counts

FROM bond AS b
JOIN connected AS c ON c.bond_id = b.bond_id
JOIN molecule AS m ON m.molecule_id = b.molecule_id
WHERE b.bond_type = '-' 
  AND c.atom_id IS NOT NULL 
  AND c.atom_id2 IS NOT NULL 
  AND c.atom_id != c.atom_id2
  AND m.label = '-'
ORDER BY b.molecule_id
LIMIT 3

  b.molecule_id

JOIN connected AS c
  ON c.bond_id = b.bond_id

WHERE
  b.bond_type = '-'

  AND NOT c.atom_id2 IS NULL
  AND c.atom_id <> c.atom_id2
  AND m.label = '-'
ORDER BY
  b.molecule_id
LIMIT 3
